text: Mostra tuttiPhotosynthesis is the process by which plants, algae, and some bacteria use sunlight, water, and carbon dioxide to create oxygen and energy in the form of sugar. It occurs primarily in the leaves within cell structures called chloroplasts, which contain the green pigment chlorophyll. Monash University +3The process operates through two main stages: 1. Light-Dependent Reactions These reactions take place in the thylakoid membranes inside the chloroplast. National Institutes of Health (.gov)Solar Absorption: Chlorophyll absorbs energy from sunlight.Water Splitting: This solar energy is used to split water molecules (H2Ocap H sub 2 cap O𝐻2𝑂) absorbed by the plant's roots.Energy Creation: Splitting the water releases oxygen (O2cap O sub 2𝑂2) as a byproduct (which is released into the atmosphere) and generates energy-rich molecules called ATP and NADPH. YouTube·Happy Learning English +52. Light-Independent Reactions (The Calvin Cycle) Also known as "dark reactions," these occur in the fluid-filled stroma of the chloroplast. National Institutes of Health (.gov) +1Carbon Intake: The plant absorbs carbon dioxide (CO2cap C cap O sub 2𝐶𝑂2) from the air through tiny pores in its leaves called stomata.Sugar Synthesis: Using the ATP and NADPH generated during the light reactions, the plant converts the carbon dioxide into sugars, such as glucose.Growth: The plant uses this glucose for energy and to build structures like cellulose, fueling its growth and development. Britannica +6The Chemical Equation The overall biochemical process is summarized by the following chemical equation:6CO2+6H2O+Light energy→C6H12O6(glucose)+6O26 cap C cap O sub 2 plus 6 cap H sub 2 cap O plus Light energy right arrow cap C sub 6 cap H sub 12 cap O sub 6 open paren glucose close paren plus 6 cap O sub 26𝐶𝑂2+6𝐻2𝑂+Light energy→𝐶6𝐻12𝑂6(glucose)+6𝑂2 For an engaging visual breakdown of how these chemical reactions interact in everyday plants:54sPhotosynthesis Explained in Less Than 2 Minutes!Utah Valley UniversityYouTube• 1 dic 2022
